story.asA: developer working in a containerized environment
story.iWant: my code changes to be automatically detected and the server to restart instantly
story.soThat: I can see the results of my changes immediately without manual intervention and maintain a fast development workflow
story.tasks:
Task 1: Configure NestJS watch mode (AC: #1, #4, #6) - Subtask 1.1: Add `start:dev` script to package.json with `--watch` flag - Subtask 1.2: Verify NestJS CLI watch mode configuration - Subtask 1.3: Test watch mode locally (outside Docker)
Task 2: Configure Dockerfile.dev for hot reload (AC: #2, #3) - Subtask 2.1: Verify volume mount: `.:/app` (source code) - Subtask 2.2: Verify node_modules NOT mounted (excluded from volume) - Subtask 2.3: Update CMD to use `npm run start:dev` (watch mode)
Task 3: Test hot reload performance (AC: #5) - Subtask 3.1: Measure restart time on file change - Subtask 3.2: Verify < 3 saniye requirement - Subtask 3.3: Optimize if necessary (tsconfig, nest-cli.json)
Task 4: Docker Compose integration (AC: #7, #8, #9) - Subtask 4.1: Test: `docker-compose up -d` → App starts with watch mode - Subtask 4.2: Edit controller file → Verify auto-restart - Subtask 4.3: Check logs: "Restarting..." message appears
Task 5: End-to-end validation (AC: #7, #8) - Subtask 5.1: Make change to existing file (e.g., add console.log) - Subtask 5.2: Verify change reflected in running app - Subtask 5.3: Test with database-dependent change (use seed data from Story 10-4)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. nest start --watch configured in package.json 2. Source code volume mount → container yapılmış (Dockerfile.dev) 3. node_modules container içinde (mounted değil) - volume exclude edilmiş 4. TypeScript compilation: Watch mode aktif 5. Server restart on file change: < 3 saniye (measured) 6. Console output: "Restarting..." mesajı görünüyor (file change detection) 7. Docker Compose command: docker-compose up -d → Hot reload çalışıyor 8. Edit source file → Server otomatik restart oluyor 9. docker-compose logs -f app → "Restarting..." mesajı görünüyor

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification: Development Environment
doc.section: Non-Functional Requirements → Performance → Hot Reload Performance
doc.snippet: Target: Server restart < 3 seconds (p95) on file change. Strategy: Incremental TypeScript compilation, Exclude node_modules from volume mount, Use Alpine base image for faster builds

doc.title: Story 10.1: Development Dockerfile
doc.snippet: Dockerfile.dev with node:20-alpine base image, working directory /app, npm ci for dependencies, volume mount support for hot reload

doc.title: Story 10.2: Docker Compose Setup
doc.snippet: Multi-service orchestration with PostgreSQL 15, MongoDB 6, Redis 7. Source code volume mount: .:/app, environment variables injected, port mappings configured

doc.title: Story 10.3: Environment Variable Management
doc.snippet: All environment variables documented in .env.example, config validation in src/main.ts ensures fail-fast pattern, multi-environment templates ready

doc.title: Story 10.4: Database Seed Scripts
doc.snippet: Docker services running and healthy, seed data with 10 users, 3 roles, 11 permissions. Docker integration working: docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Test coverage requirements: 70% minimum enforced by CI/CD. Unit tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern with proper mocking and verification

doc.title: PRD - Non-Functional Requirements & Coding Standards
doc.snippet: Code quality standards: TypeScript strict mode, ESLint configuration, consistent naming conventions, modular architecture with clear separation of concerns

artifact.reason: Container definition for hot reload development. CMD already configured: npm run start:dev (watch mode). Volume mount strategy: .:/app for source code, /app/node_modules excluded
Multi-service orchestration. App service: volume mount ..:/app, command: npm run start:dev, depends_on postgres/mongodb/redis. Environment variables properly injected
start:dev script: nest start --watch (line 12). Docker scripts: docker:up, docker:down, docker:logs, docker:exec for container management
Application entry point. Config validation enabled (fail-fast). Health check endpoint available at /health for container health monitoring

constraints.constraint: File change → server restart: < 3 seconds (measured, p95)
Source code volume mount: .:/app (bidirectional sync for hot reload)
node_modules: EXCLUDED from volume mount (prevents native module conflicts)
Base image: node:20-alpine (Alpine Linux, ~100MB vs ~900MB Debian)
Command: npm run start:dev (NestJS watch mode with file watching)
Database services: Independent (no restart when app restarts)
Environment variables: Injected via docker-compose env_file
Prisma Client: Regenerated at startup (reflects mounted schema changes)

tests.standards: Hot reload validation follows NestJS watch mode best practices with manual testing approach. Docker container startup: < 10s, File change restart: < 3s (p95), TypeScript compilation: incremental, < 1s. Test coverage requirements: 70% minimum enforced by CI/CD. Unit tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern with proper mocking. Integration tests use separate test database. All tests must pass before merge. Volume mount strategy validated to ensure node_modules not affected by file changes.
tests.locations: test/ (unit tests), test/integration/ (integration tests), test/e2e/ (end-to-end tests). Hot reload tests are manual validation: docker-compose up -d, edit file, measure restart time. Test files use .test.ts extension. Mock factories available in test/factories/ directory. Database tests use test database with clean setup/teardown.

test.method: Test: npm run start:dev locally. Verify: Server starts with watch mode, console shows file watching active. Location: Terminal output, package.json script validation.
Test: docker-compose up -d, then docker-compose exec app ls -la node_modules. Verify: node_modules exists in container, not mounted from host. Edit src/ file, verify change reflected immediately.
Test: docker-compose up -d, edit src/controller.ts, time from save to 'Restarting...' in logs. Verify: < 3 seconds. Measure multiple times for p95. Location: docker-compose logs output.
Test: docker-compose up -d, edit any .ts file. Verify: Server restarts automatically, no container restart. Location: docker-compose logs -f app
Test: docker-compose logs -f app while editing files. Verify: 'Restarting...' message appears on each file change. Location: Container logs output.
Test: After hot reload, query existing data (seed users from Story 10-4). Verify: Database connection persists, no data loss. Location: Application logs, database query results.
